causal_chains.description: Braking too early causes low apex speed
Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 1 (54.1% – 57.8% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.315s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 775.9 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Braking too early causes low apex speed (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: You can brake later and carry more speed into the corner — trust the car's braking capability.
    → Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application (confidence: 70%)
    💡 Tip: Low mid-corner speed is tempting you to get on throttle early. Focus on carrying more apex speed instead.
zones.llm_feedback: You're carrying 14.5 km/h more speed than the expert through this segment, which is a great start. However, you're also braking 2.1% more than the expert, which is likely causing you to lose momentum. To improve, try braking 2.1% less and aim to be closer to the expert's braking point, which should help you maintain a better apex speed. Additionally, be mindful of your throttle application, as you're currently at -3.0% compared to the expert, and focus on smoothing it out to avoid rear instability.

causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed
Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex

zones.template_feedback:

      Deviation: 694.9 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 70%)
    💡 Tip: Get on the throttle earlier and more decisively on corner exit to maximise straight-line speed.
    → Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex (confidence: 7%)
    💡 Tip: Try initiating your braking earlier and with more initial pressure to scrub speed before the turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback: You're running 694.9 rpm lower than the expert reconstruction, which is likely costing you corner exit speed. To improve, focus on getting on the throttle earlier and being more aggressive with your application - aim to match the expert's RPM. Additionally, consider fine-tuning your braking point, as you're currently braking 0.2% less than the expert, which may be contributing to a slightly slower corner exit. By making these adjustments, you should be able to carry more speed through the turn and improve your overall lap time.

summary_llm: The largest area for improvement lies in optimizing corner entry and exit speeds, with all zones showing significant time loss due to low RPM. To address this, focus on delaying braking by a fraction of a second, particularly in Zone 1 where braking too early results in being 775.9 rpm under optimal RPM. Despite these challenges, it's encouraging to see that the driver is consistently pushing to the limit, with an overall severity of 100% indicating a strong willingness to take risks and improve. By making these adjustments, the driver can work to shave off the estimated total time loss of ~0.771s and take their performance to the next level.
